---
title: "CV"
---

<div class="full-bleed-banner-cv">
<img src="images/CV-banner.jpg" class="cv-banner">
</div>

## Current position

**Technical Officer for Marine Policy and Advocacy**  
**SEO/BirdLife**, Madrid, Spain  
**November 2025 – present**

I work on marine policy and advocacy across maritime sectors in Spain, with a focus on biodiversity-compatible offshore wind, fisheries, and marine spatial planning.

---

## Professional experience

**Technical Officer for Marine Policy and Advocacy**  
**SEO/BirdLife**, Madrid, Spain  
**November 2025 – present**

- Policy and advocacy work across maritime sectors in Spain  
- Focus on offshore wind, fisheries, and marine spatial planning  
- Science-informed support for biodiversity-compatible marine governance  

**Research Technician**  
**Universitat de Girona (UdG)**, Girona, Spain  
**November 2022 – March 2025**

- Worked on the BIOPAIS project, focused on the ecological and social impacts of offshore wind development in areas of high biodiversity and landscape value, particularly in Cap de Creus / Gulf of Roses

**Research Technician**  
**CEAB-CSIC (Centre for Advanced Studies of Blanes)**, Blanes, Spain  
**November 2020 – September 2022**

- Worked on DIVERSAT, a project focused on biodiversity status and trends in Spanish littoral reefs  
- Participated in MAGNET project, including scientific communication activities for students  


**Master’s Thesis Internship**  
**CEAB-CSIC**, Blanes, Spain  
**January 2020 – July 2020**

- Master’s thesis: *How healthy are our reefs? A quantitative approach to measure global reef health using benthic marine invertebrates*

**Bachelor’s Thesis Internship**  
**Réserve naturelle de la baie de l’Aiguillon**, France  
**June 2017 – August 2017**

- Bachelor’s thesis on the monitoring of the ecological functions of salt meadows for ichthyofauna

---

## Education

**PhD in Marine Sciences**  
**Universitat de Barcelona (UB)**  
**2023 – 2025**

- Thesis: *Ecological impacts of floating offshore wind development in the Mediterranean in the context of the climate and biodiversity crisis*

**Master’s degree in Science and Technology, specialization in Marine Sciences**  
**Aix-Marseille Université (AMU)**  
**2019 – 2020**

**Bachelor’s degree in Science and Technology, specialization in Life and Earth Sciences**  
**Aix-Marseille Université (AMU)**  
**2017 – 2018**

**Bachelor’s degree in General Biology**  
**La Rochelle Université**  
**2016**

---

## Additional training

**S4D4C European Science Diplomacy Online Course**  
**European Union**  
**2022**

**MARINE ECOMED – intensive study programme (Erasmus+)**  
**Aix-Marseille Université**  
**2019**

- Training linked to Maritime Spatial Planning and Mediterranean marine governance

---

## Research stays

**Plentziako Itsas Estazioa (PiE), University of the Basque Country (UPV/EHU)**  
Plentzia, Spain  
**October 2021**  
Research stay in marine ecology

**Plentziako Itsas Estazioa (PiE), University of the Basque Country (UPV/EHU)**  
Plentzia, Spain  
**May 2021**  
Research stay in marine ecology

---

## Academic service and leadership

**Representative of Marine Science doctoral students**  
Doctoral Commission, Universitat de Barcelona  
**June 2024 – December 2025**

**Representative of doctoral students**  
Gender Equality Commission, Faculty of Earth Sciences, Universitat de Barcelona  
**August 2023 – December 2025**

**Evaluation Committee Member**  
**PRISMA Conference 2024/2025/2026**  
Asociación PRISMA

---

## Teaching and supervision

- Supervision of an undergraduate dissertation on the impacts of offshore wind on marine mammals in the Mediterranean  
  (**Universitat de Girona**) 
- Supervision of an Erasmus+ undergraduate project on the impacts of offshore wind on ecosystem services provided by marine habitats  
  (**Universitat de Girona / Université de Monastir**)

---

## Policy and stakeholder processes

- Participation in ministerial and stakeholder workshops related to Spain’s Maritime Spatial Plans (POEM)  
- Participation in the LIFE INTEMARES workshop for the development of the management plan of the SCI *Sistema de cañones submarinos occidentales del golfo de León*

---

## Languages

- **French** — C2
- **English** — C1
- **Catalan** — C1
- **Spanish** — C1

---

## Certifications and field qualifications

- **Two-star scuba diver** — CMAS  
- **One-star scuba diver** — CMAS  
- **RIFAP (Reactions and intervention in diving accidents)** — CMAS  
- **Occupational risk prevention in aquatic and underwater activities** — CSIC  

---

## Volunteering

**Communications Officer (Board Member)**  
**PRISMA – Asociación para la Diversidad Afectivo-Sexual y de Género en Ciencia, Tecnología e Innovación**  
**May 2024 – present**
